---
title: "Effects of BMAA on interoception, alexithymia, and empathy among adults with high autistic traits"
author: "Yang, C.-J."
image: "../../assets/study-covers-en/2024-yang-autistic-traits.png"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

```{r}
#| include: false
source("../../R/bmaa_plot_theme.R")
```

::: {.study-cover}
<img src="../../assets/study-covers-en/2024-yang-autistic-traits.png" alt="Expression recognition">

Cover figure: after BMAA, low-intensity facial-expression recognition improved.
:::

## Method

A pretest-posttest control-group study with adults scoring high on autistic traits. The BMAA group received a four-week, 12-hour program plus additional home practice.

## Key Findings

The main change was not an immediate drop in autistic traits themselves, but improvement in abilities that may support social-emotional functioning. Interoception means sensing internal bodily signals; alexithymia means difficulty identifying and describing emotions; low-intensity facial-expression recognition captures sensitivity to subtle social cues.

The BMAA group showed increased interoception, reduced alexithymia, and improved low-intensity expression recognition, while the control group changed less.

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4.8
dat <- data.frame(
  outcome = rep(c("Interoception (MAIA-C)", "Alexithymia (TAS-20)", "Low-intensity expression recognition"), each = 4),
  group = rep(c("BMAA", "BMAA", "Control", "Control"), 3),
  time = rep(c("Pretest", "Posttest", "Pretest", "Posttest"), 3),
  value = c(81.12, 96.65, 80.73, 82.18, 58.71, 50.53, 56.36, 55.91, 40.44, 45.51, 40.22, 40.40)
)
bmaa_facet_line_plot(dat, "BMAA and interoception, alexithymia, and subtle expression recognition", ylab = "Mean score")
```

## Reference

楊捷如. (2024). *身心中軸覺察對於高自閉症特質成年人之內感覺、述情障礙及同理心的影響* [碩士論文，中原大學].
